news.title: Federal appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pill mifepristone
news.summary: Mifepristone, involved in most abortions in the U.S., can now be distributed only in person and at clinics.

news.title: Opinion: STAT readers on MAHA activists, perimenopause, and diversity in medical school
news.summary: “Unfortunately, the resources don't exist to take every flat-earther to the International Space Station to change their mind,” a STAT reader writes.

news.title: Opinion: What do medical students think about their education?
news.summary: “No one's pulling the pieces together”: a conversation with medical students about preventive care education.

news.title: Cleveland Clinic taps startup Luminai to test how AI can run hospital operations
news.summary: As the Cleveland Clinic works with Luminai, tech leadership sees opportunities to use its tech to tackle other administrative workflows, especially in workflows that are high-volume, complex and operationally intensive, Rohit Chandra, Cleveland Clinic chief digital officer, said.

news.title: Senators introduce clean extension to cost-based payments for some rural hospitals
news.summary: The bipartisan reauthorization would add five more years to the Rural Community Hospital Demonstration, a payment model for hospitals with fewer than 51 beds but still too large for Critical Access Hospital designation.

news.title: Industry Voices—Value-based care won the policy argument. Now it has to deliver
news.summary: Value-based care has succeeded in changing incentives. The next phase must focus on building the operational capacity required to deliver on those incentives.

news.title: Health Tech Weekly Rundown: Sage launches Tasking for senior care workflows; St. Luke’s taps Auxira Health for cardiologist support
news.summary: Stay up-to-date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Beth Israel Lahey Health taps Heidi for system-wide AI scribe rollout
news.summary: The scaled deployment follows a six-month trial for 1,000 providers at the Boston-based health system.

news.title: Omada signs on with Optum Rx's GLP-1 management program
news.summary: Omada Health has signed on with Optum Rx to participate in its Weight Engage program, furthering the company's ability to scale its offerings to reach more people who need cardiometabolic care and support.

news.title: Who do Americans believe have the most influence in healthcare?
news.summary: A new survey report explores the disconnect between who Americans consider influential in healthcare and who they wish had greater influence. The delta was greatest for clinicians.

news.title: Tenet Healthcare met Q1's volume curveballs with 'old-fashioned discipline'
news.summary: With much of the quarter's volume disruptions sequestered to January and early February, CEO Saum Sutaria, M.D., said Tenet was able to regain ground by adjusting its expenses to better reflect demand, rebooking ambulatory procedures and “focusing more on some of our other types of work in the hospitals.”

news.title: Education Department finalizes industry-opposed student loan caps
news.summary: The final rule excludes post-baccalaureate health roles like advanced practice registered nurses and physician associates from higher borrowing caps permitted for "professional" degrees, like physicians. Healthcare groups had warned it could hamstring the clinical workforce pipeline.

news.title: Trump pulls surgeon general nomination of Casey Means, names Nicole Saphier as new pick
news.summary: The president blamed Senate HELP Chair Bill Cassidy for blocking the confirmation of Means, an influential voice within the Make America Healthy Again movement. His new choice is a practicing radiologist, Fox News medical contributor and an author previously critical of the government's COVID-19 pandemic response.

news.title: DOJ launches West Coast Health Care Strike Force to target fraud in Arizona, Nevada, Northern California
news.summary: The Department of Justice has formed a new fraud strike force to target schemes across Arizona, Nevada and Northern California, the agency announced Thursday.

news.title: Cigna to exit ACA market, pursue strategic alternatives for eviCore unit
news.summary: The Cigna Group beat the Street on both earnings and revenue in the first quarter of 2026, posting $1.65 billion in profit for Q1.

news.title: Scaling theranostics through access, safety and trust
news.summary: Curium’s Mike Patterson on improving access, safety and trust to scale theranostics and enhance patient care.

news.title: ‘M&A used to be the only way to go’: Why Seaport’s CEO opted for route to upsized $255M IPO
news.summary: "We want to develop those programs as far as we can ourselves,” Seaport CEO Daphne Zohar told Fierce.

news.title: Summit's PD-1xVEGF interim trial miss surprises analysts, shares tumble
news.summary: A surprise interim trial miss in ivonescimab’s closely watched Harmoni-3 trial has sent Summit Therapeutics’ shares tumbling, casting a shadow over the company’s momentum as the star PD-1xVEGF bispecific heads into the ASCO annual meeting with a prestigious spot on the plenary session.

news.title: CBER chief Prasad steps aside, deputy takes the reins as search for successor continues
news.summary: Katherine Szarama, Ph.D., will take over as CBER director in an acting capacity while the search for a permanent director continues.

news.title: Ligand tries to disembark from part of Viking pact, alleging contract breach
news.summary: Viking Therapeutics’ collaboration with Ligand Pharmaceuticals has sailed into some trouble, with one of the partners keen to jettison part of the pact.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Astellas appoints new top strategist from Sanofi Japan
news.summary: After last month’s departure of longtime executive Adam Pearson, Astellas has picked out a new strategist to fill the open spot on its C-suite with Takahiko Iwaya, who takes over as chief strategy officer on July 1.

news.title: J&J axes $5B CAR-T dream months after touting best-in-disease efficacy
news.summary: Johnson & Johnson has dropped a pair of CAR-T cell therapies, axing programs it once predicted could deliver peak sales north of $5 billion.

news.title: Genomics inks partnership deal with Greywolf to assess gene datasets
news.summary: Genomics inked a partnership deal with fellow U.K.-based biotech Greywolf Therapeutics to organize and evaluate ERAP enzymes and how they influence autoimmune disease risks.

news.title: Amgen abandons candidates for cancer, Sjögren’s syndrome over weak ph. 2 data
news.summary: Amgen has pulled the plug on midstage drug candidates for cancer and the autoimmune disease Sjögren’s syndrome, terminating five clinical trials in the process.

news.title: AstraZeneca's camizestrant ambitions stumble as FDA panel rejects novel oral SERD proposal
news.summary: An FDA expert panel on Thursday voted 6 to 3 against AstraZeneca's proposal of using its oral SERD camizestrant in a novel first-line switch setting in HR-positive, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer, dealing an early blow to the British pharma's $5 billion peak sales plan.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Autolus decreases headcount by 13%; Replimune reduces staff
news.summary: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: BMS calls time on Zymeworks collab, ending work on phase 1 cancer bispecific
news.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has called time on a bispecific antibody partnership with Zymeworks that the Big Pharma inherited from Celgene.

papers.title: Safety Drift After Fine-Tuning: Evidence from High-Stakes Domains
papers.summary: Foundation models are routinely fine-tuned for use in particular domains, yet safety assessments are typically conducted only on base models, implicitly assuming that safety properties persist through downstream adaptation. We test this assumption by analyzing the safety behavior of 100 models, including widely deployed fine-tunes in the medical and legal domains as well as controlled adaptations of open foundation models alongside their bases. Across general-purpose and domain-specific safety benchmarks, we find that benign fine-tuning induces large, heterogeneous, and often contradictory cha

papers.title: ClawGym: A Scalable Framework for Building Effective Claw Agents
papers.summary: Claw-style environments support multi-step workflows over local files, tools, and persistent workspace states. However, scalable development around these environments remains constrained by the absence of a systematic framework, especially one for synthesizing verifiable training data and integrating it with agent training and diagnostic evaluation. To address this challenge, we present ClawGym, a scalable framework that supports the full lifecycle of Claw-style personal agent development. Concretely, we construct ClawGym-SynData, a diverse dataset of 13.5K filtered tasks synthesized from pers
